extra.reasoning:
The property (never rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b jointly true) holds across the entire 16-state input space.

rec_drug_a: Only r1 (IF elderly THEN rec_drug_a := true, priority 50) affects it; otherwise default false. So rec_drug_a = true iff elderly = true.

rec_drug_b: Two rules conflict on the same trigger (elderly): r2 (THEN rec_drug_b := true, priority 50) and r4 (THEN rec_drug_b := false, priority 90). Per-output conflict resolution gives the highest-priority matching rule the win, so r4 (90) always beats r2 (50) when elderly is true, setting rec_drug_b := false. When elderly is false, neither r2 nor r4 fires, so the default sets rec_drug_b := false. Therefore rec_drug_b is false for every input.

Since rec_drug_b is identically false, the conjunction (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is false for all inputs. The only candidate for a violation would require rec_drug_a = true (which needs elderly = true), but in exactly that case r4's higher priority forces rec_drug_b = false. No reachable input makes both true. Property holds.
